text.title: django-base-model Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-base-model as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Exposing auto-increment id in DRF serializers or URL patterns instead of uid.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leaks table size, enables enumeration attacks, and couples public API to internal DB layout. Always use uid in Meta.fields and set lookup_field = 'uid' on viewsets.

failure-mode.description: Overriding Meta in a child model without inheriting BaseModel.Meta (class Meta(BaseModel.Meta)).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silently loses the base ordering (['-created_at']) and get_latest_by ('created_at'); discovered only when querysets return unexpected order in production or when a manager calls .latest() and gets the wrong row.

failure-mode.description: Using default=uuid.uuid4() (called once at import time) instead of default=uuid.uuid4 (callable).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All rows created after the first migration get the same UUID. Caught only by a test that creates two rows and asserts u1.uid != u2.uid.

failure-mode.description: Applying BaseModel to M2M through-tables and intermediate models.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Through-tables are link entities, not domain entities. Extra UUID column and unique index balloon the index count for zero benefit. Reserve BaseModel for domain models only.

failure-mode.description: Squashing migrations without verifying the UUID unique=True constraint is preserved.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-squash can drop and re-add constraints without backfilling — silently corrupts production data if the constraint is dropped without running a backfill. Never auto-squash; always review the generated migration file.
